channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BC Ferries workers face challenges maintaining aging fleet amid delays and breakdowns
item.description: Employees of BC Ferries are working around the clock to maintain an aging fleet, as the company faces cancellations and delays due to mechanical issues and scheduled maintenance. The BC Ferry and Mari...

item.title: Mitsubishi to start producing hybrid cars in the Philippines
item.description: Mitsubishi has announced its plans to begin manufacturing hybrid cars in the Philippines, a move that could potentially boost the local automotive industry and promote sustainable mobility in the coun...

item.title: Nigeria Leads Africa in Food Waste, EU Raises Concern Over Environmental and Economic Impacts
item.description: Nigeria is currently the largest contributor to food waste in Africa, with an estimated 38 million tonnes of food wasted annually, a situation the European Union (EU) has described as alarming. The EU...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV's First Easter Blessing Calls for Peace in the Middle East Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: In his first Easter blessing as pontiff, Pope Leo XIV called on world leaders, particularly those with the power to wage war, to choose peace as the Middle East remains embroiled in conflict. The war,...

item.title: Home Office launches programme to support schools in high-risk areas against knife crime
item.description: The Home Office in England has announced a £1.2 million initiative to provide targeted support to schools at greatest risk of knife crime. The programme, part of a broader government pledge to halve k...

item.title: Experiencing a Week of High-Protein Diet: Challenges and Observations
item.description: A WIRED journalist experimented with a week-long diet consisting solely of high-protein foods, following guidance promoted by RFK Jr. and new federal dietary recommendations emphasizing protein intake...

item.title: Safety Concerns Raised After Stabbing at Edmonton Hospital Emergency Department
item.description: An incident at an Edmonton hospital's emergency room has sparked renewed discussions about safety protocols for both patients and staff. A stabbing occurred within the ER, resulting in injuries that r...

item.title: Jammu & Kashmir loses over 300 lakes, contributing to flood risks
item.description: Jammu and Kashmir has experienced a drastic loss of nearly 3,000 hectares of lakes since 1967, as per a recent Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) report. The state initially had 367 lakes in Jammu ...

item.title: Regional Differences in U.S. Warming Patterns Revealed by Study
item.description: A recent study published in PLOS Climate reveals that climate change impacts across the United States are far more uneven than previously thought. While only about half of U.S. states have recorded an...

item.title: Goa Businessman's Son Arrested for Car Accident That Killed Hotel Employee in Dona Paula
item.description: A tragic accident in Goa’s Dona Paula locality on Sunday night claimed the life of 23-year-old Diksha Parwadkar, a hotel employee, and seriously injured her colleague. The incident occurred when a Min...

item.title: BPF Removes Himanta Biswa Sarma's Photo from Posters in Certain Assam Constituencies
item.description: In Assam, the Bodoland People’s Front (BPF) has recently modified its election campaign posters, removing Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma’s photo in areas dominated by Muslim voters. The change fol...

item.title: Alex Eala to Compete Against Julia Grabher in Linz Open First Round
item.description: Filipino tennis star Alex Eala is set to make her debut at the Linz Open, a professional women's tennis tournament held in Austria. In her first-round match, she will face Austria's Julia Grabher. Eal...

item.title: Washington Woman Sentenced to Nearly 28 Years for Planned Murder of Father
item.description: Alyssa Bradburn, 33, was sentenced to 340 months in prison for the 2024 murder of her father, Timothy Bradburn, in Spokane, Washington. The court found that she had meticulously planned the killing fo...

item.title: Australia's Fuel Supply: Rationing Concerns and Government Assurance
item.description: Australia is facing growing fears over fuel rationing, particularly with supply disruptions tied to global events like the ongoing US-Iran conflict. While the Federal Government, through Energy Minist...

item.title: Trump criticises allies over limited support in Iran conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has held a fiery news conference criticising several allied nations, including Australia, Japan, South Korea, and NATO, for not providing enough support in the conflict with ...

item.title: Major General Majid Khademi, Iran’s Revolutionary Guards Intelligence Chief, Killed in US-Israeli Airstrike
item.description: In a significant development amidst the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran, Major General Majid Khademi, the chief of intelligence for Iran’s Revolutionary Guards, was killed in a US-Is...

item.title: Georgia’s 2026 Senate Contest Begins as Republicans Seek to Unseat Ossoff
item.description: The 2026 Georgia Senate race is beginning to heat up as Democratic Senator Jon Ossoff faces the prospect of tough opposition from Trump-aligned Republicans. Politico reports that several potential GOP...

item.title: Iran rejects US pressure, says Strait of Hormuz won’t reopen for temporary ceasefire
item.description: In the latest developments from the ongoing conflict in West Asia, Iran has firmly stated that it will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a temporary ceasefire, following a proposal by Pa...

item.title: Trump threatens military action against Iran if peace deal is not reached
item.description: US President Donald Trump has warned that the military is ready to destroy all bridges and power plants in Iran within a four-hour period if Iran does not agree to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesd...

item.title: UCLA Wins First NCAA Women’s Basketball Championship After Defeating South Carolina
item.description: UCLA secured its first NCAA women’s basketball championship with a decisive victory over South Carolina in the Final Four of the tournament held on April 5, 2026, in Phoenix. The Bruins displayed a do...
